key_people.name: Chang Won-sam (정원삼)
key_people.title: President (제14대 협력단장)
key_people.title_description: Chief executive of KOICA, appointed by the President of South Korea upon recommendation of the Foreign Minister. Three-year term.
key_people.description: Career diplomat with over 40 years of experience in foreign affairs and public office. Former Secretary General of the Northeast Asian History Foundation (NAHF). Assumed office on July 10, 2023 as the 14th President of KOICA.

departments.name: Office of the President (단장실)
departments.head: Chang Won-sam
departments.description: Executive office of the KOICA President, providing strategic direction and oversight of all agency operations

departments.name: Planning & Coordination Bureau (기획조정실)
departments.description: Strategic planning, budgeting, policy coordination, and organizational management

departments.name: Development Cooperation Bureau (개발협력실)
departments.description: Oversees bilateral project operations across sectors: education, health, governance, agriculture, technology, and energy

departments.name: World Friends Korea (WFK) Program Office (월드프렌즈한국 사업단)
departments.description: Manages overseas volunteer dispatch programs unified under the WFK brand since 2009, similar to the US Peace Corps

departments.name: Fellowship Program Office (CIAT) ( CIAT사무처)
departments.description: Manages the KOICA Scholarship/Fellowship Program offering Master's and Doctoral degree programs to professionals from partner countries

departments.name: Humanitarian Assistance & Disaster Relief Division (인도적지원재난구호)
departments.description: Emergency humanitarian aid and disaster relief operations in crisis-affected regions

departments.name: Multilateral Cooperation Division (다자협력)
departments.description: Partnerships with international organizations including UN agencies, World Bank, and multilateral development banks

departments.name: Global Disease Eradication Fund (세계질병퇴치기금)
departments.description: Manages the levy-funded program collecting 1,000 KRW per international flight departure to support infectious disease eradication and maternal-child health in sub-Saharan Africa

departments.name: Civil Society Partnership Division (시민사회협력)
departments.description: Partnerships with Korean and international civil society organizations and NGOs for development cooperation

recent_events.title: KOICA expands development cooperation with Vietnam
recent_events.description: President Chang Won-sam visited Hanoi from April 22-25. KOICA signed an MoU with Hyundai Motor Company and Vietnam's Ministry of Education and Training for the 'Vietnam Industrial Workforce Development Program (2026-2030)' to train automotive sector technicians. Chang also signed a memorandum of discussion with Vietnam's science and technology minister for joint R&D partnership (2024-2033), and reviewed inclusive education and smart library projects.
recent_events.impact: Major trilateral partnership (industry-government) linking industrial workforce development, science and technology collaboration, and education in Vietnam.

recent_events.title: KOICA allocates $14 million for IT education in Uzbekistan
recent_events.description: KOICA launched a $14 million grant project in Uzbekistan to strengthen IT education, expand digital skills training, and support youth employment and entrepreneurship through new IT hubs, training programs, and startup incubation initiatives.
recent_events.impact: Expands KOICA's digital transformation agenda in Central Asia.

recent_events.title: Hyundai Motor Group-KOICA-Vietnam MoU for automotive workforce development
recent_events.description: Hyundai Motor Group signed an MoU with KOICA and Vietnam's Ministry of Education and Training to develop skilled technical workforce for Vietnam's automotive sector. The program runs from H2 2026 through 2031, focusing on die-casting, press forming, and welding training curricula. Graduates will be connected with employment at Vietnamese SME component manufacturers.
recent_events.impact: Pioneering trilateral industry-government partnership for sector-specific skills development in Vietnam's automotive industry.

recent_events.title: President Chang visits Laos to review ODA projects
recent_events.description: President Chang Won-sam visited Laos from February 5-7 to review progress of ongoing ODA projects and discuss future development cooperation strategies with Lao government officials.
recent_events.impact: Strengthening bilateral development ties with Laos.

recent_events.title: KOICA President attends Korea-Timor-Leste Friendship Sports Center inauguration
recent_events.description: President Chang attended the inauguration of the Korea-Timor-Leste Friendship Sports Center, built under the 'Sports for Children Development Project in Timor-Leste (2021-2025)' with a total budget of USD 6.5 million.
recent_events.impact: Completion of major sports-focused ODA project in Timor-Leste.

recent_events.title: KOICA President meets Bangladesh Chief Adviser Muhammad Yunus
recent_events.description: President Chang Won-sam met with Chief Adviser Professor Muhammad Yunus in Dhaka. KOICA committed $96 million to five major projects in Bangladesh spanning advanced technology, AI, human resources, and sustainable rural development beginning in 2026.
recent_events.impact: Substantial expansion of KOICA's development portfolio in Bangladesh.

recent_events.title: KOICA signs $19 million ODA agreements with Tajikistan
recent_events.description: KOICA and Tajikistan signed agreements for two ODA projects totaling $19 million aimed at enhancing agricultural resilience to climate change in the Rudaki and Hisor regions.
recent_events.impact: Expanding climate-focused ODA in Central Asia.

recent_events.title: KOICA launches $10 million Youth Resilience Project in Ethiopia
recent_events.description: KOICA and Ethiopia's Ministry of Women and Social Affairs signed a Record of Discussion for a $10 million grant project titled 'Building Adaptive Resilience for People Affected by Conflict and Climate Crisis Focusing on Youth' in the Afar and Somali regions.
recent_events.impact: New humanitarian-development nexus project in East Africa.

related_entities.org_description: Parent ministry overseeing KOICA; the Foreign Minister recommends the KOICA President for appointment

Japanese counterpart development agency; regular cooperation and knowledge exchange
Korea joined DAC in 2010; KOICA operates under DAC frameworks and ODA reporting standards
Strategic partner with programmatic alignment across 27 priority partner countries
KOICA is an accredited national direct access entity of the GCF
Unified overseas volunteer brand managed by KOICA since 2009, encompassing seven volunteer programs

core_business: 韩国国际协力团（KOICA）是韩国政府于1991年4月1日设立的正部级政府开发援助（ODA）执行机构，隶属外交部，总部位于京畿道城南市（825 Daewangpangyo-ro, Sujeong-gu, Seongnam-si）。作为韩国官方无偿援助和技术合作的核心执行机构，KOICA在全球46个国家设有驻外事务所，员工约379人。核心业务涵盖：（1）双边开发合作项目，覆盖教育、卫生、治理、农业与农村发展、技术与能源、跨领域议题等六大领域；（2）World Friends Korea（WFK）海外志愿者派遣项目，自2009年统一整合七个部委的志愿者项目以来已累计派遣超过20,000名志愿者；（3）CIAT奖学金/研修生项目，每年为来自伙伴国家的政府官员和专业人才提供约100-300个全额资助的硕士及博士学位名额；（4）人道主义援助与灾难救助（HADR）；（5）多边合作，与联合国各机构、世界银行等多边组织开展联合项目；（6）全球疾病消灭基金，通过向韩国出发的国际航班征收每张1,000韩元的附加费，资助撒哈拉以南非洲地区的传染病防治和母婴健康项目；（7）民间社会合作（CSP）及创新开发项目（DEEP）。韩国2022年ODA总额约28亿美元，占国民总收入（GNI）的0.17%。KOICA制定数字化转型战略（2021-2025）及科技创新中期战略（2021-2025），推动数字要素在所有ODA项目中的主流化。现任第14任总裁张元三（Chang Won-sam）于2023年7月10日就任，任期为三年。
apec_stance: KOICA不直接参与APEC政策制定，但在APEC框架下的人道主义援助、能力建设、教育合作、数字化转型和技术转让等领域可作为韩国政府的执行机构发挥重要作用。作为韩国ODA的核心执行机构，KOICA的项目覆盖多个APEC成员经济体（越南、印尼、菲律宾、秘鲁、智利、墨西哥等），通过双边项目、奖学金项目和志愿者派遣深化韩国与APEC发展中经济体的合作关系。2026年与Hyundai Motor Group及越南教育部签署的三方汽车产业人才培养MoU，体现了将产业合作融入ODA的新模式。

collection_meta.notes: Enriched from phase 2 skeleton. Key data: President Chang Won-sam (since 2023-07-10), 46 overseas offices, 379 employees, $2.8B Korea ODA (2022). Major 2026 events: Vietnam workforce MoU with Hyundai, $14M Uzbekistan IT education, $96M Bangladesh commitment. Anti-pattern check passed: no family_members, no placeholder data, no smart quotes, all dates valid.
